# We Need More Real World Applications in COMP 110 
by: Brynna Myers 

## My interests
I am currently in COMP 110 because it is a requirement for my Neuroscience major, but I was excited to take this class because I know that technlogy and coding is a part of our everyday lives. 
I plan on attending PA school in the future where I am aware that for my capstone master thesis coding my be in my future, but I felt like this was never addressed in the class.
So I set out to see if other students felt the same - that comp 110 was interesting even thoguh they weren't planning on pursuing a degree in this school. 

## My idea 
By cumulating and displaying this data, I hope to prove that non-computer science majors are very engaged and interested in this major, and therefore, the instructors should include a few mini lectures or real world coding assignments that can be related to a different discipline. 

## My Analysis
Using Python, I analyzed survey responses from COMP110 students in the Spring 2026 Alyssa Lytle section taking note of three keyy questions: how interested they are in connections between CS and other fields? Whether they find the course interesting? Whether they find the skills valuable?

## My Analysis 
# Finding 1
Visualization 1: An overwhelming majority of students find the Connections of Computer Science interesting and applicable to other fields. Most students answered high in the 5/6 agree and strongly agreeing of of a 7 point scale. This information is extremely supportive of my hypothesis that students find the application of computer science interesting and applicable to other fields, so it would only be logical that students would want to see these application sof crossing Computer Sceince to other fields in the class since they have already intuitively seen the connection.
# Finding 2 
 Visualizatoin 2: In general there is an upward trend and most students who find the class interesting also see the value of the skills they are learning. However, we see that the distribution of these majors are across many different disciplines demonstarting this course is interesting and valuable to many people. From this fiure it would be plausible to assume that students would really appreciate adn value seeing more rela world and diverse applications of computer sceince. For example, there are many physics, chemistry, and business majors who find this course interesting and valuable, so it would be beneficial if some of the assignments where structured with examples from these disciplines. 
# Finding 3
 Visualization 3: An interest in computer science connections spans across many majors. The stacked histogram shows that high scores on our 1-7 scale come from majors ranging in Biology, Human Development, Applied Siences, and Economics. This diversity of majors at the higher level interest siggest that this course should have cross discipline examples because they would resonate broadly and help maintain and appeal to a large majority of the class.

## Conclusion 
The data from my analysis supports that it would be ebenficial for the instructors to add mroe real world application examples and problems to help meet the extremely diverse yet itnerested student population. Including examples and datasets would help increase and already very prevelant engagment and percieved value of the course, but it would just solidify what students are already pulling from this class.
